_meta.note: GA legislature batch: blake-tillery (R SD-19 — ran for Lt. Gov, LOST 2026 GOP primary; sitting senator, strong-conservative sponsor record), larry-walker-iii (R SD-20 — President Pro Tem, establishment-conservative, RFRA+SB390 sponsor, no deviations), joseph-gullett (R HD-19 — TPAction 90/100, Zuckerbucks-ban + DA-oversight sponsor), charlice-byrd (R HD-20 — Freedom Caucus chair; freedomindex.us data DISCOUNTED after verification flipped 3 votes [SB233 actually YES, HB428 unanimous, SB140 excused] — scored from primary sponsorships + iVoterGuide self-statements only), brad-thomas (R HD-21 — sworn Jan 2021, FastDemocracy-confirmed votes). State tier. Evidence-first: party-line marquee GA votes cited as HARD for the recorded side where individual roll calls confirmed or candidate is sitting R; iVoterGuide questionnaire answers treated as self-stated TRUE/FALSE per the social-media protocol; nulls where no evidence; no conservative deviations found.

set.office: State Senator (sitting · ran for Lt. Governor, lost 2026 GOP primary)

profile.background: GA State Senate District 19 (Vidalia/Toombs County) since 2017; chairs Senate Appropriations and the Special Committee on Eliminating Georgia's Income Tax. Strong-conservative legislator: prime sponsor of SB39 (2025 ban on state-plan gender-affirming care) and SB21 (2025 anti-sanctuary), co-sponsor of SB36 (RFRA), SB202, SB390, SB420.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored from his iVoterGuide questionnaire (self-stated positions), authored/sponsored bills, FastDemocracy-confirmed roll calls (SB140, SB233, HB1105, SB63), and GA party-line marquee votes. CANDIDACY UPDATE: he did NOT seek SD-19 re-election — he ran for Lieutenant Governor and LOST the May 19, 2026 GOP primary (~19%, did not advance to the Kennedy-vs-Dolezal runoff). Continues serving SD-19 through Jan 2027. NOTE: not certified by Georgia Life Alliance (2022 or 2026 lists) and no GA Right to Life endorsement on record — life cells rest on his iVoterGuide answers, not a pro-life-group rating.

blake-tillery@GA.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-20. Consistent Georgia conservative (96% Turning Point Action lifetime, 98% Faith & Freedom); no deviations found. Life: iVoterGuide affirms 'legal protection from conception until natural death,' opposes PP funding; supported HB481 (party-line R). Marriage/sexuality: iVoterGuide one-man/one-woman; PRIME SPONSOR SB39 (state-plan gender-care ban); YEA SB140 (minor gender-care ban); co-sponsor SB390 (anti-ALA library). Family: YEA SB233 vouchers, authored pro-voucher op-ed. Christian liberty: co-sponsor SB36 (RFRA), iVoterGuide conscience-exemption support. Elections: YEA + co-sponsor SB202 (photo ID, drop-box/Zuckerbucks restrictions). Border: YEA HB1105, PRIME SPONSOR SB21 (anti-sanctuary), co-sponsor SB420 (foreign farmland). 2A: SB319 const-carry (anti-Dem-amendment vote), NRA 'A' (2016). Justice: YEA SB63 (cash bail). Several cells null (no documented position).

profile.background: GA State Senate District 20 (Perry/Houston County) since 2015; elected President Pro Tempore (#2 chamber role) Jan 12, 2026. Establishment/pro-business conservative (Robins AFB, rural hospitals, income-tax cuts). Running for re-election to SD-20 in 2026.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored from his authored/co-sponsored bills (SB36 RFRA sponsor, SB202 co-sponsor, SB390 chief sponsor) and GA party-line marquee votes where he served as a sitting Republican. Individual roll calls on HB481/SB140/SB233/SB319/HB1105/SB420/SB63 could not be pulled (LegiScan/Vote Smart 403-blocked); scored per the marquee-party-line rule. No NRA/Georgia Life Alliance rating surfaced; no abortion-industry money found. No deviations despite his establishment reputation.

Conservative voting record (Turning Point Action 90% 2026 / 100% lifetime key-issues). Confirmed running UNOPPOSED in the 2026 GOP primary.

profile.background: GA House District 20 (Cherokee County/Woodstock); current tenure since Jan 2021 (earlier service 2005-2013); chairs the Georgia Freedom Caucus. Hardline-conservative legislator who SPONSORS, not just votes: HB441 (Prenatal Equal Protection / abolition-personhood), HB293/HB121 (Second Amendment Preservation Act), HB313 (Ten Commandments in schools), and a resolution to impeach Fulton DA Fani Willis. Running for re-election 2026.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-20 — evidence-scored from her PRIMARY-sourced bill sponsorships (legiscan/news), iVoterGuide questionnaire (self-stated positions), GRTL endorsement and GGO/NRA ratings. CRITICAL METHOD NOTE: freedomindex.us (John Birch 'Freedom Index') data was DISCOUNTED for her after verification flipped three of its vote labels — it showed SB233 as a NO (actually a confirmed YES, House Vote #697, 2024), HB428 as a NO (the bill passed UNANIMOUSLY 172-0/160-0, a NO was impossible — and she CO-SPONSORED IVF protection), and SB140 as a YES (the primary roll call shows her EXCUSED/not voting). All index-derived votes are therefore excluded; only primary-sourced sponsorships, confirmed roll calls, and self-statements are scored. No conservative deviations found.

charlice-byrd@GA.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-20. Georgia Freedom Caucus chair; scored from primary sources after discounting unreliable freedomindex.us data (which falsely showed deviations on SB233/HB428/SB140). Life: PRIMARY SPONSOR of HB441 (2025 Prenatal Equal Protection Act — abolition-of-abortion personhood-from-fertilization), rally quote 'at conception the baby would have the same rights'; GRTL-endorsed; GLA-certified 2020/2022. Note she also CO-SPONSORED HB428 (Right to IVF), so the embryo-discard cell is left null. Marriage: iVoterGuide one-man/one-woman, rejects SOGI protected-class. Family: CONFIRMED YEA on SB233 vouchers (2024, primary roll call); iVoterGuide opposes CRT. Christian liberty: CO-SPONSOR of HB313 (Ten Commandments in schools); iVoterGuide gospel-freedom. Elections: iVoterGuide photo-ID, one-day voting, eliminate drop boxes. Border: iVoterGuide border wall + deny funds to non-compliant entities. 2A: CHIEF SPONSOR of the Second Amendment Preservation Act (HB293/HB121); Georgia Gun Owners '100% Rock Solid'; NRA-PVF endorsed. Federal overreach: SAPA is a 10th-Amendment anti-commandeering vehicle. Justice: filed resolution to IMPEACH DA Fani Willis (rogue-prosecutor). SB140 was EXCUSED (not scored); HB267/HB1105 individual votes unconfirmed (scored from self-statements instead). No deviations found.
